texto: We've been very lonely, but we had it easy. Because death is so heavy - we, too young to know about it, couldn't handle it. After this you and I may end up seeing nothing but suffering, difficulty and ugliness, but if only you'll agree to it, I want for us to go on to more difficult places, happier places, what ever comes, together. I want you to make the decision after you're completely better, so take your time thinking about it. In the mean time, though, don't disappear on me.

texto: It’s a marvelous thing, the ocean. For some reason when two people sit together looking out at it, they stop caring whether they talk or stay silent. You never get tired of watching it. texto: Living like that utterly convinced me of the extreme limitations of language. texto: I was frightened of so many things, in my vanity, that ultimately i couldn't protect myself any other way. Try not to be like that, okay? texto: We ran into lots of old friends. Friends from elementary school, junior high school, high school. texto: I felt sure of this. However much I loved him, and as beautiful as the world was, none of it was powerful enough to take the weight off his heart, that heaviness that dragged him down, into the beyond, making him yearn to be at peace.
explicacao: Citação de Banana Yoshimoto sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “I felt sure of this. However much I loved him, and as beautiful as the world was, none of it was …” articula amor, depression, love em registro de amor, com relevância para contextos como love.

texto: When was it I realized that, on this truly dark and solitary path we all walk, the only way we can light is our own? Although I was raised with love, I was always lonely.Someday, without fail, everyone will disappear, scattered into the blackness of time.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Banana Yoshimoto que provoca vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. Ao questionar “When was it I realized that, on this truly dark and solitary path we all walk, the only way we ca…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre amor, death, vida, com foco em luto e reflexao-noturna.

texto: I’ll never be able to be here again. As the minutes slide by, I move on. The flow of time is something I cannot stop. I haven’t a choice. I go. One caravan has stopped, another starts up. There are people I have yet to meet, others I’ll never see again. People who are gone before you know it, people who are just passing through. Even as we exchange hellos, they seem to grow transparent. I must keep living with the flowing river before my eyes.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Banana Yoshimoto sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “I’ll never be able to be here again. As the minutes slide by, I move on. The flow of time is some…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a vida, banana-yoshimoto, growth, útil em momentos de mudanca e growth.

One caravan has stopped, another starts up. There are people I have yet to meet, others I’ll never see again. People who are gone before you know it, people who are just passing through. Even as we exchange hellos, they seem to grow transparent. I must keep living with the flowing river before my eyes.

texto: I never tell my boyfriend that I'm busy when I'm not. No matter how effective they are, cheap techniques like that just don't agree with me. So it's always okay, it's always all right. In my opinion the surest way to hook a man is to be as open with him as possible.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Banana Yoshimoto: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “I never tell my boyfriend that I'm busy when I'm not. No matter how effective they are, cheap tec…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, relationships, always pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e reflexao.
